info.title: Prudra API
info.description:
The Infrastructure Layer for Enterprise AI Agents.

**Base URL:** `https://api.prudra.dev`

**Authentication:** All endpoints require a Bearer token.
Pass your API key in the `Authorization` header:
`Authorization: Bearer prv_live_sk_...`

Use `prv_test_sk_...` keys for development.
Test keys accept `PAYMENT_STUB_MODE` payments.

ApiKeyAuth.description: Your Prudra API key. Get one at dashboard.prudra.com/settings/api-keys

detail.example: Your plan includes 100 payments per month. You have used 100.

usage.description: Current usage vs plan limits for each metric

tags.name: Wallets
tags.description: Managed wallet provisioning and BYO wallet registration

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Account profile and organisation payment logs

tags.name: Organisation wallet
tags.description: Legacy Tempo receiving wallet (single wallet per org)

get.description: Usage for the current billing period against plan limits, plus enabled features.
Plan name, feature flags, and limit values (lighter than /billing/usage for some clients).

post.description: Rebuilds Redis usage counters from Postgres for the authenticated organisation.
The raw key is returned once — store it securely. Caller must be a member of the organisation.

key.description: Raw API key — shown once only

delete.description: Only owner or admin roles may revoke. Members receive 403.

post.description:
Generates both an MPP (WWW-Authenticate) and x402 (PAYMENT-REQUIRED)
challenge for a pay-walled endpoint. Called internally by the Prudra
Express SDK's payMiddleware — developers do not call this directly.

Rate limited to 20 requests per IP per 60 seconds.

422.description: Wallet not found or not in this organisation

post.description:
Verifies an x402 or MPP payment credential in the request body.
Creates a Payment record on success. For x402, returns a settlementToken for use
with POST /payments/settle after work is completed.

credential.description: Raw payment credential (x402 X-PAYMENT payload or MPP proof)

post.description:
Called after the server has completed its work following a successful
POST /payments/verify. Submits the ERC-3009 authorization to chain.
Moving funds from the agent wallet to the receiving wallet.

Only used for x402. MPP is settled at verification time.

get.description: Paginated payment activity for the authenticated organisation (query params passed through to the service).
Aggregated stats per pay-walled route for the organisation.

post.description:
Internal endpoint used by the SDK to register observed pay-walled routes and metadata
for the authenticated organisation.

Creates a new payment session and its linked vault atomically.
Called by payMiddleware after a successful payment when session
mode is enabled.

429.description: Session payments not available on plan

get.description:
Retrieves an existing payment session by ID, scoped to the
authenticated organisation.

post.description:
Creates a new vault — a persistent workspace for agent task output.
TTL defaults from server config; use ttl for a custom duration (e.g. 24h, 7d).

get.description: Plan limits and current vault usage for the organisation.
Full vault state and manifest (requires vault access token or API key per middleware).

delete.description: Permanently removes the vault and associated contents.

post.summary: Upload a file to a vault
post.description:
Accepts multipart form data with field `file`. Returns upload instructions / result
(pre-signed URL flow) for storing content in object storage.

429.description: File too large or storage limit reached

post.summary: Add a document to a vault

post.description: Records an event on the vault for streaming and history.

Permanently closes the vault to further writes.
A sealed vault is read-only.
Opts the vault into permanent storage so it no longer expires.

409.description: Vault is already sealed or persisted

post.description: Short-lived token for vault-scoped access (e.g. for clients without the org API key).

get.description:
Server-Sent Events stream for vault activity. Optional query `from` (ISO timestamp)
replays events after that time.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Replay events after this ISO timestamp

get.description: Returns the legacy single-wallet record for Tempo / stablecoin payouts (not wallet-infra managed wallets).

post.description: Idempotently provisions the org's legacy Tempo wallet for the given coin.

get.description: Live stablecoin balance for the org receiving wallet.

post.description: Queues a stablecoin withdrawal from the org wallet to an EVM address.

HTTPS endpoint for outbound events. The response may include a `secret` once
for HMAC verification (X-Prudra-Signature on deliveries).

secret.description: Optional custom signing secret (min length enforced by API)

post.description:
Registers an existing EVM wallet address for Prudra monitoring.
Prudra never holds custody — it monitors deposits via webhooks.

delete.description: Stops monitoring the address for this organisation.

get.summary: List supported tokens for a chain

200.description: Token metadata for routing and display

post.description:
Called by Alchemy with webhook payloads. Not authenticated with Prudra API keys —
Alchemy signs requests; the server acknowledges immediately and processes asynchronously.

Provisions a new managed master wallet for the organisation.
Prudra generates and custodies the private key using Google Cloud KMS
(HSM-backed). Each organisation uses a master seed from which
wallet addresses are derived on each supported chain.

429.description: Managed wallet or chain limit reached

post.description: Derives a new child address from a master wallet using HD derivation.

Transfers tokens from a managed wallet (master or child) to a destination
address. Routing (direct transfer, swap, bridge) is selected automatically from
fromChain, fromToken, toChain, and toToken.

Initiates a withdrawal from a managed wallet toward a registered bank account.
Converts on-chain balance to fiat per your integration settings.
